extract._comment_downloadDirectory: Where raw PDF files are saved (archival only). NOT indexed by mcp-local-rag.
extract._comment_papersDirectory: Where parsed Markdown (.md) files are saved. This is the BASE_DIR for mcp-local-rag. Agent uses ingest_file / query_documents on files here.

headlessBrowser._comment_browser: Options: 'msedge', 'chrome'. Patchright requires a Chromium-based browser. GRaDOS probes paths for the configured browser type on the current OS (Windows/macOS/Linux).
headlessBrowser._comment_executablePath: Optional absolute or PROJECT_ROOT-relative browser executable path override. Use this if GRaDOS cannot find the configured browser.

parsing._comment_markerWorkerDirectory: Optional Marker worker directory override. Point this to the marker-worker folder itself (the directory containing worker.py, install.sh/install.ps1, and local.env after installation), not to its parent directory. Relative paths resolve from PROJECT_ROOT.
parsing._comment_markerTimeout: Maximum time in milliseconds to wait for the local Marker worker process. If markerWorkerDirectory is set, GRaDOS uses it directly; otherwise it falls back to PROJECT_ROOT/marker-worker (if present) and then PACKAGE_ROOT/marker-worker (default: 120000).

localRag._comment_enabled: Set to false to skip local paper library steps in the agent workflow (Step 0 in SKILL.md). The mcp-local-rag server must still be separately registered in your MCP client.
localRag._comment: Reference config for mcp-local-rag. Current 0.10.x requires Node.js 20+. Install: npx -y mcp-local-rag. For direct local-rag:* calls to share the same index as GRaDOS search_saved_papers, keep BASE_DIR/DB_PATH/CACHE_DIR/MODEL_NAME aligned with the MCP client config shown in the README.
localRag._comment_baseDirShouldMatch: Set mcp-local-rag's BASE_DIR env var to the same absolute path as extract.papersDirectory above.
localRag._comment_dbPath: Optional but recommended. Keep this aligned with the companion mcp-local-rag DB_PATH so GRaDOS search_saved_papers and direct local-rag tools see the same semantic index.
localRag._comment_cacheDir: Optional model cache directory for both mcp-local-rag and GRaDOS's built-in semantic saved-paper search.
localRag._comment_modelName: Optional embedding model for mcp-local-rag CLI usage. Changing it requires rebuilding the local RAG index.
localRag._comment_command: Optional CLI command GRaDOS should use when search_saved_papers attempts semantic retrieval through mcp-local-rag.
localRag._comment_args: Optional CLI args paired with localRag.command above.

playwrightMcp._comment: Optional companion: Playwright MCP provides LLM-friendly browser automation as a fallback when the built-in headless browser fails. Install: npm install -g @playwright/mcp. Register: claude mcp add playwright -- npx @playwright/mcp --headless
playwrightMcp._comment_usage: When extract_paper_full_text fails, the agent can use Playwright MCP tools (browser_navigate, browser_snapshot, browser_click) to adaptively navigate publisher pages and download PDFs, then call parse_pdf_file to parse them.

zotero._comment: Zotero Web API settings. Get your API key at https://www.zotero.org/settings/keys and your library ID at https://www.zotero.org/settings/keys (shown as 'Your userID for use in API calls').
zotero._comment_libraryId: Your Zotero numeric user ID (not username). Found at zotero.org/settings/keys.
zotero._comment_libraryType: 'user' for personal library, 'group' for shared group library.
zotero._comment_defaultCollectionKey: Optional. Zotero collection key to file papers into by default. Leave empty to save to library root. Get a key from your Zotero library URL.

_comment_academicEtiquetteEmail: Email used for academic APIs (Crossref, Unpaywall) to identify requests and get into their polite pools. CHANGE THIS to your real institutional email.
